creator: amdmi3@amdmi3.ru
text:
* b. f. (bf1783@googlemail.com) wrote:

> It does not seem necessary or desirable to add such clutter to
> bsd.gcc.mk, as there are already less-intrusive ways to do this,
> because the variables in question don't contain full paths (partly for
> this reason, in fact).  For example, one can simply place
> /usr/local/libexec/ccache in the PATH before /usr/local/bin, or add
> the desired symlinks or wrappers in ~/bin, as is typical practice with
> a masquerading compiler.

I didn't think of $PATH, so you're right - I'll close the PR then.

--
